notes:
EX-era set (2004-08-30). Pack = 9 cards confirmed two ways: Bulbapedia Booster-pack page states the entire EX Series had 9-card boosters and 36-pack boxes; FRLG booster product listing (TCGplayer/ToyWiz via search) gives '5 Commons, a Reverse Holo, a Rare-or-better, and 2 Uncommons' = 5+2+1+1 = 9. Modeled exactly that way.

Available buckets {rare-holo:17, rare:14, uncommon:36, common:36, ex:9, secret-rare:4}. Intentionally NO gold-star bucket: Gold Stars debuted in EX Team Rocket Returns (the NEXT set), so FRLG has zero Gold Stars (Bulbapedia FRLG page lists only Common/Uncommon/Rare/Rare Holo/Rare Holo ex/Rare Secret). The 4 secret rares = Charmander 113, Articuno ex 114, Moltres ex 115, Zapdos ex 116 (legendary-bird ex's).

Rare-or-better slot resolves across {rare, rare-holo, ex, secret-rare}, sums to exactly 1.0:
- ex=0.083 (~1:12 packs, ~3/box). Flipside Gaming (Part 1, aggregating Pokebeach/Pokegym) sources the EX-era ex band as 1:36 (Holon Phantoms, scarce end) to 1:8-12 (Power Keepers, generous end). FRLG is mid-era and ex-heavy (9 ex's), so placed toward the generous middle.
- secret-rare=0.009 (~1:108, ~1 per 3 boxes for the 4 secrets combined). Flipside notes EX-era secret rares sit below the ex rate in frequency.
- rare-holo=0.333 (~1:3 packs, ~12 holos/box). Era ~1:3 holo convention; cross-checks the community '~12+ rares per box' figure (12/36=0.333).
- rare=remainder=0.575.

Reverse-holo slot modeled with plain common/uncommon ids per dataset convention (value is what matters); neutral 50/50 split given equal 36/36 common/uncommon pools.

Confidence LOW: no FRLG-specific published pull-rate study; rates are EX-era community/retailer averages mapped onto the set. Vintage sealed boxes carry a heavy collector premium so EV/booster sits far below box price — expected, rates not inflated.

VALIDATION: file written to data/pull-rates/ex-firered-leafgreen.json; `npm run validate:data` reports 'All datasets valid'. All slot distributions sum to 1.0; slot card-counts total 9 = packSize; all distribution keys are a strict subset of the available buckets.
